name: Royal Kunia
desc: Newer subdivision near Kunia — affordable, family-oriented, between Waipahu and Schofield, limited services.
insider: Kunia Road is the local shortcut to the North Shore — it bypasses H-2 traffic on weekends and connects to Whitmore Village and Haleiwa
The neighborhood is surrounded by active pineapple fields and agricultural plots — the views of rows of red earth and green plants at sunset are stunning
There's essentially one main way in and out via Kupuna Loop — it feels self-contained and very private
Agricultural operations still surround the neighborhood — roadside fruit stands on Kunia Road sell fresh pineapple, papaya, and seasonal produce direct from the fields
Schofield Barracks is just up the road — military families make up a large portion of the community and turnover is real
Kunia Country Farms sometimes hosts seasonal events and U-pick opportunities — great for families with kids
There's no shopping within Royal Kunia itself — Waipahu Town Center or Waikele Outlets are your closest options, both about a 10-minute drive
The community park has a nice playground and pavilion but no pool — for swimming you'll head to Mililani or Waipahu rec centers
Homes here were mostly built in the 1990s-2000s and are well-priced compared to Mililani — expect $700K-850K for a 3-bedroom
The elevation is just high enough to catch decent trade winds — not as breezy as Makakilo but cooler than the Ewa coastal flats
